2605.09579.title: Biosignal Fingerprinting: A Cross-Modal PPG-ECG Foundation Model
2605.09579.paper_summary: Cardiovascular disease remains the leading cause of global mortality, yet scalable cardiac monitoring is hindered by the gap between diagnostic-rich ECG and ubiquitous wearable PPG. Bridging this gap requires representations that are compact, transferable across modalities and devices, and deployable without task-specific retraining. Here we introduce biosignal fingerprints: compact latent representations of cardiovascular state derived from a cross-modal foundation model, the Multi-modal Masked Autoencoder (M2AE), trained on over 3.4 million paired ECG and PPG signals. M2AE integrates modality-specific encoders with a shared bottleneck and dual decoders, jointly optimized using reconstruction and cross-modal contrastive objectives, yielding generalizable fingerprints that retain intra- and inter-modality features. Like a biometric fingerprint, these representations uniquely encode an individual's cardiovascular state in a modality-agnostic, privacy-preserving form reusable across clinical tasks without exposing raw waveform data or requiring model retraining. Across 7 downstream tasks, spanning cross-modal reconstruction, cardiovascular disease classification, hypertension detection, mortality prediction, and demographic inference, biosignal fingerprints achieve competitive or superior performance compared to leading domain-specialist foundation models in frozen settings, including an AUROC of 0.974 for five-class CVD classification and 0.877 for hypertension detection, with a maximum improvement of 27.7% in AUROC across 5 classification tasks. Critically, strong performance is maintained with only a single modality, enabling deployment in resource-constrained, single-sensor environments typical of real-world wearable monitoring, with direct implications for continuous cardiovascular monitoring across clinical and consumer health settings.
2605.09579.paper_summary_zh: 心血管疾病仍然是全球死亡的主要原因，但可擴展的心臟監測受到診斷豐富的心電圖（ECG）與無處不在的光電容積描記（PPG）之間的差距所阻礙。彌補這一差距需要緊湊的表示，這些表示能夠跨模態和設備轉移，並且可以在不進行特定任務重新訓練的情況下部署。在這裡，我們介紹生物信號指紋：這是一種來自跨模態基礎模型的心血管狀態的緊湊潛在表示，該模型是多模態掩碼自編碼器（M2AE），其訓練基於超過340萬對的ECG和PPG信號。M2AE整合了特定模態的編碼器，並具有共享的瓶頸和雙解碼器，通過重建和跨模態對比目標進行聯合優化，產生可泛化的指紋，保留了模態內和模態間的特徵。就像生物識別指紋一樣，這些表示以模態無關、保護隱私的形式獨特地編碼個體的心血管狀態，可重複使用於臨床任務，而無需暴露原始波形數據或進行模型重新訓練。在7個下游任務中，涵蓋跨模態重建、心血管疾病分類、高血壓檢測、死亡率預測和人口推斷，生物信號指紋在冷凍設置中實現了與領先的領域專家基礎模型相比具有競爭力或更優的性能，包括五類心血管疾病分類的AUROC為0.974和高血壓檢測的AUROC為0.877，在5個分類任務中AUROC的最大改進達到27.7%。關鍵是，即使僅使用單一模態，強大的性能也得以維持，這使得在資源受限的單傳感器環境中部署成為可能，這在現實世界可穿戴監測中是典型的，對於臨床和消費者健康設置中的持續心血管監測具有直接的影響。
2605.09579.author: Zhangdaihong Liu et.al.
2605.09579.authors: Zhangdaihong Liu, Chang Liu, Fenglin Liu, Yixuan Chen, Yang Yang, David A. Clifton, Xiao Gu
